prompt: What is priority inversion? Describe a scenario where busy waiting (spinlock) can lead to it. How can priority inheritance solve this issue?
reference_answer:
a. Priority inversion occurs when a higher-priority task is blocked by a lower-priority one.
b. Scenario: Low-priority A holds a lock; high-priority B spins waiting. The scheduler runs B over A, preventing A from releasing the lock.
c. Priority inheritance solves this by temporarily boosting the priority of the lock holder (A) to the level of the waiting high-priority task (B).
